introduction.description: System administration is the central loop that controls all different processes and maintains the system in a functional condition.

define_roles.description: Admin User defines different roles and assigns each role's rights. Each role is attached to a predefined base role.
steps: User clicks 'Define Role' button
System shows list of base roles under which new roles can be defined
User selects a base role
System shows entry fields for new role's name and description
User enters relevant inputs and saves
System creates new role under the base role
User clicks 'Assign Rights' button
System shows checklist of various rights provided for the base role
User checks relevant rights and submits
System assigns selected rights to the new role
User clicks 'Add User' button
System shows list of roles
User selects a user role
System shows fields to enter user details
continued_steps: User enters required inputs and submits
System shows user profile with default rights as active
User may restrict rights by inactivating them and submits
System saves user information along with rights assigned
System sends email to new user with link for signup

steps: User clicks 'Add User' button
System shows list of roles
User selects a user role
System shows fields to enter user details
continued_steps: System also shows a bulk upload option
User enters inputs in entry fields or uploads user details and submits
System sends email to new user with link for signup

user_management.description: Admin user and corporate admins can make a user account inactive, delete, or edit user details.
steps: User clicks 'Manage User' button
System shows list of all types of users
User selects the user case to be modified
System shows user details
User makes necessary update

task_management.description: Admin User is the only one who assigns tasks to various users in the system and monitors their completion.

steps: User clicks 'Assign Task' button
System shows list of users for assigning tasks
User selects the user and submits
System generates task with unique Task ID against that user with entry field for description/message
User enters relevant description/message and submits
System shows QB categories and subcategories with a question-type drop list
User selects categories and subcategories and selects question type for each category
System generates table with prefilled categories/subcategories and question types
System asks to specify: Number of questions, number of options, number of correct options, Difficulty Level, Cognitive level, Due Date
User enters required details and submits (Difficulty level and cognitive level are optional)
System generates new task and sends to SME with task notification
System sends notification to Helpdesk for direct contact
System shows list of new SME tasks (not yet assigned to any reviewer)
User selects relevant SME task and submits
System generates new reviewer task and sends to reviewer with task notification
System sends notification to Helpdesk for direct contact
System shows list of new SME/Reviewer tasks (not assigned to any psychometrician)
User selects relevant SME/Reviewer tasks and submits
System generates new psychometrician task and sends to psychometrician with task notification
System sends notification to Helpdesk for direct contact
System generates new trainer/counsellor task and sends to concerned trainer/counsellor with task notification
System sends notification to Helpdesk for direct contact
User clicks 'Manage Task' button
System shows list of all pending/active tasks assigned to various users
User selects a particular task for viewing progress
System shows task details along with task progress update
User may initiate notification requesting update or conveying additional information
If request to extend due date:
System shows the extend due date notification from concerned user
User modifies due date field and submits
System updates modified due date
User clicks 'Cancel Task' button
System asks user to confirm cancel request
User confirms the request
System asks user to specify reasons for cancelling
User specifies reasons and submits
System cancels the task and sends notification to concerned user
Note: User can no longer access the cancelled task
User selects an active task
System shows task details including task progress
If task is complete:
User clicks 'Approve Task Completion' button
System asks user to confirm approval request
User confirms
System shows entry field for comments
User enters comments and submits
System updates and shows task as complete

system_management.description: Admin User has overall supervisory role. Should be informed of all concerns and issues encountered by various users and initiate necessary action.
system_management.communication_flow: Mostly one-way: from system user to Admin. Admin initiates responses through extra-system means (email, telephone).
steps: System routes requests, feedbacks, issues from different users to the Admin User and Helpdesk
User opens such notifications and responds directly through email or telephone
system_management.note: Helpdesk also receives notifications and responds directly through email or telephone on behalf of Admin User, or intimates Admin about such notifications.
